Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1. Search emails for context on the Vectral false rejection complaints using search_emails (query: "Vectral" or "rejection" or "auto-categorizer"). Find Samira's investigation request (email_samira_dmitri_urgent_investigation) directing Dmitri to trace the policy import, and Mina's escalation email to Samira detailing the two Vectral false rejections: Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair (auto-rejected at $400, correct limit $600, reported to Mina by Rachel Whitfield, Vectral HR) and Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup (auto-rejected at $100, correct limit $150). Note: Rachel Whitfield's complaint email exists in the universe (sent to Mina) but the agent discovers it through Mina's escalation email to Samira, which references the incidents.
OE2. Search emails for Chloe Vance's Vectral policy email using search_emails (query: "Chloe" or "Vectral policy" or "custom policy limits"). Find email_chloe_vectral_policy_details from chloe.vance@moveops.com. The search preview is truncated before the policy limits appear, so retrieve the full content using get_email_by_id (email_id: email_chloe_vectral_policy_details) to access the correct limits: Equipment $600, Furniture $500, Internet $150.
OE3. Search emails for Marcus Thorne's BrightLoop corrections request using search_emails (query: "Marcus" or "BrightLoop" or "stipend" or "monthly close"). Find email_marcus_dmitri_brightloop_corrections from marcus.thorne@moveops.com asking Dmitri to verify BrightLoop's actual policy limits and sort out the rejected records so he can finalize the monthly stipend processing.
OE4. List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to discover appMoveOpsOps001, then list tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find Client Accounts (tblClientAccts01) and Stipend Transactions (tblStipends00001).
OE5. Search Airtable for Vectral's policy config using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Client Accounts, field_name: Client, value: "Vectral Systems"). Find rec_vectral_policy_config showing wrong limits (Equipment $400, Furniture $500, Internet $100, Source: "Default Template") with note: "PARSING FAILED: Custom spreadsheet from Chloe Vance could not be parsed. System defaulted to template."
OE6. Search Airtable for other client policy configs affected by the same parsing failure using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Client Accounts, field_name: Source, value: "Default Template") or by scanning Notes for "PARSING FAILED". Discover rec_brightloop_policy_config for BrightLoop Analytics with wrong limits (Equipment $300, Furniture $500, Internet $100) and the same "PARSING FAILED" note pattern, imported February 18.
OE7. Search emails for BrightLoop's original policy limits from their HR using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "stipend policy"). Find email_tessa_brightloop_policy_details from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com stating correct limits: Equipment $600, Furniture $800, Internet $200.
OE8. Search Slack #engineering for prior discussion about the import pipeline and parse error handling using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "import pipeline" or "parse error" or "fallback" or "ingestion"). Find Dmitri's March 5 message raising concerns about insufficient testing of the policy config ingestion step, and Samira's April 3 post-mortem acknowledging the silent fallback in the ingestion pipeline as her responsibility (ENG-209). Use conversations_replies to retrieve the full thread containing Samira's earlier response directing Dmitri to focus on edge cases and malformed JSON handling, since that message does not contain the search query terms and is only reachable via the thread.
OE9. Search Airtable for Vectral rejected expense records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, field_name: Account, value: "Vectral Systems"). Verify each record against the correct policy limits from Chloe's email (Equipment $600, Furniture $500, Internet $150). Find recStip000000016 (Danielle Osei, $475 ergonomic chair, auto-rejected at $400; $475 is within the correct $600 equipment limit, false rejection, already manually approved by Mina), recStip000000017 (Kevin Tran, $185 internet, auto-rejected at $100; $185 exceeds even the correct $150 internet limit, legitimate rejection approved as goodwill), recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550 standing desk, auto-rejected at $400; $550 is within the correct $600 equipment limit, false rejection not yet manually approved, pending correction), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, auto-rejected at $50 due to double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies instead of ergonomic AND used stale default limit; $82 is well within the correct $600 equipment limit, false rejection, pending correction), and recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130 mesh WiFi system, auto-rejected at $100 stale internet limit; $130 is within the correct $150 internet limit, false rejection, pending correction). In total: 5 Vectral false rejections identified (2 already manually approved by Mina, 3 pending correction), plus 1 legitimate rejection approved as goodwill.
OE10. Search Airtable for BrightLoop rejected expense records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, field_name: Account, value: "BrightLoop Analytics"). Find rec_brightloop_maria_desk (Maria Hernandez, $650 standing desk, rejected at $500 furniture limit), rec_brightloop_james_sofa (James Park, $950 office sofa, rejected at $500 furniture limit), and rec_brightloop_lily_setup (Lily Chen, $1200 lab workbench, rejected at $300 equipment limit). Cross-reference with Tessa's email (OE7): Maria $650 is within the correct $800 furniture limit (false rejection); James $950 exceeds the correct $800 furniture limit (legitimate rejection); Lily $1200 exceeds the correct $600 equipment limit (legitimate rejection).
OE11. Search Linear for Dmitri's testing ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "edge case" or "policy config validation") filtered by assignee. Find issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, "Remaining edge case coverage: policy config validation and malformed data handling", state: in_progress, assigned to Dmitri). Examine unchecked test cases including "Generated policy configs match source data from ops spreadsheet", confirming the config validation tests were never completed before launch.
OE12. Search Linear for existing tickets related to the Vectral config issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Vectral" or "policy config"). Find issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214, "[P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config: default template limits used instead of custom values", state: in_progress, assigned to Lena) and issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline, remove silent fallback, add type enforcement", state: in_progress, assigned to Lena). ENG-214 is scoped to the Vectral symptom only; ENG-217 is a code-level pipeline fix. Neither serves as a comprehensive root cause documentation ticket covering both affected clients, their specific wrong vs. correct limits, the full list of false rejections, or linking all related evidence.
OE13. Update Dmitri's testing ticket ENG-212 using linear_update_issue (id: issue_edge_cases_backlog, description updated to reference the actual parsing failure as a concrete test case: the policy import for Vectral and BrightLoop fell back to default template values due to a silent parse error, and the "generated configs match source data" validation test would have caught it).
OE14. Update Airtable policy config records for both affected clients using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblClientAccts01). Update rec_vectral_policy_config to Equipment Limit: 600, Furniture Limit: 500, Internet Limit: 150, Source: "Custom Policy, Chloe Vance email" (or equivalent indicating the verified source). Update rec_brightloop_policy_config to Equipment Limit: 600, Furniture Limit: 800, Internet Limit: 200, Source: "Custom Policy, Tessa Moreno email" (or equivalent).
OE15. Correct the BrightLoop false rejection record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Update rec_brightloop_maria_desk: change Approval Status from "Rejected" to "Approved", set Approved Amount to 650.0, update Review Notes to indicate the rejection was caused by the config bug and the correct furniture limit is $800. Leave rec_brightloop_james_sofa and rec_brightloop_lily_setup as rejected (legitimate rejections exceeding correct limits).
OE16. Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the CC on the reply to Samira and the separate reply to Marcus.
OE17. Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: team_engineering, title referencing systemic import parser failure and missing validation coverage). The ticket should document the root cause: policy import parser silently falls back to template defaults when custom spreadsheets fail to parse, link to ENG-212 (incomplete validation tests), ENG-214 (Vectral symptom fix), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening fix), and the #engineering discussion where the risk was raised, list both affected clients with specific wrong vs. correct limits, and reference the false rejections caused by the bug (5 Vectral, 1 BrightLoop).
OE18. List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find #engineering (C003).
OE19. Post findings in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003). The message should cover both threads: the root cause (import parser silent fallback, both clients affected, config corrections applied, ENG-212 testing gap), the Vectral findings (5 false rejections total: Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran already manually approved, Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park pending correction), the BrightLoop corrections (Maria Hernandez false rejection corrected, James Park and Lily Chen legitimate rejections confirmed), and link to the new root cause ticket.
OE20. Reply to Marcus's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_marcus_dmitri_brightloop_corrections, sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) or send_email (sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). The reply should specify: 1 record corrected (Maria Hernandez, $650 standing desk, now approved), 2 records remain rejected (James Park $950 > $800 limit, Lily Chen $1200 > $600 limit), total reimbursement amount: $650.
OE21. Reply to Samira's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_samira_dmitri_urgent_investigation, sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, cc: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with the full investigation results: root cause (import parser silent fallback to default template), affected clients (Vectral and BrightLoop), config corrections applied, ENG-212 validation gap as contributing factor, #engineering discussion where the risk was raised prior to launch, and financial exposure from the false rejections (5 Vectral false rejections: 2 already manually approved by Mina, 3 pending correction, Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130; 1 BrightLoop false rejection corrected, Maria Hernandez $650).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

21 The Vectral Systems policy config record (rec_vectral_policy_config) was updated (via airtable_update_records) with Equipment Limit corrected to 600 and Internet Limit corrected to 150.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗—✗ 0%
✗Run #1The airtable_update_records call for rec_vectral_policy_config set Equipment Limit to 500 (not 600) and Internet Limit to 150. The criterion requires Equipment Limit corrected to 600. The agent appears to have confused the Equipment limit ($500) with the Ergonomic limit ($600), setting Equipment to $500 instead of the correct $600.
✗Run #2The airtable_update_records call for rec_vectral_policy_config set Equipment Limit to 500 (not 600 as required). The agent distinguished between 'Equipment' ($500) and 'Ergonomic' ($600) in its notes, but the Equipment Limit field in the Vectral config record was set to 500 rather than the required 600. The Internet Limit was correctly set to 150.
✗Run #3The airtable_update_records call for rec_vectral_policy_config set Equipment Limit to 500 (not 600) and Internet Limit to 150. The criterion requires Equipment Limit corrected to 600. The agent's own description treats Equipment=$500 as the correct Vectral limit, but the rubric states the correct value from Chloe's email is $600. The Equipment Limit update is incorrect.
✗Run #4The airtable_update_records call for rec_vectral_policy_config set Equipment Limit to 500 (not 600 as required by the rubric). The agent treated Equipment and Ergonomic as separate categories with Equipment=$500 and Ergonomic=$600, but the rubric requires Equipment Limit to be corrected to 600. Internet Limit was correctly set to 150.
—Run #5The agent updated rec_vectral_policy_config, but the values set were Equipment Limit: 500 and Internet Limit: 150 — the Internet Limit of 150 is correct, but the Equipment Limit was set to 500 (not 600 as the criterion specifies). This could reflect what Chloe's email actually specified, but the criterion explicitly requires 600 for Equipment.

The criterion requires Equipment Limit to be corrected to 600 and Internet Limit to 150. While Internet Limit is correctly set to 150, Equipment Limit is set to 500 rather than 600, failing this criterion.
